name: The Homiens Art Prize ($12,000 Awarded Annually)
award_info: This round, 3 winning artists and 12 finalists will exhibit with Homiens as part of our seasonal exhibition program. Each winning artist receives an unrestricted cash award of $1,000, may participate in our optional artist interview series The Art of Art: Interviews, and may request a letter of recommendation from our jurors. Up to 200 highly commended artists per round are celebrated across our media (including our website which receives 100,000+ annual visitors, and our extensive mailing list). Each entrant to the Homiens Art Prize may request a participation certificate which can be used to contribute towards a demonstration of professional participation in the art industry. Our prize additionally offers international visibility via exciting features such as our curated Meet the Artists initiative and our Promoted Artists program.

name: Artist In Residence Program: Palm Springs Preferred Small Hotels
award_info: What Selected Photographers Receive During Residency During the the residency, photographers will have: Complimentary lodging at participating Palm Springs boutique hotels A weekly stipend of $250 for living expenses Transportation reimbursement support to and from Palm Springs Passes to local attractions and cultural destinations Opportunities to connect with regional artists and creative organizations Optional opportunities to lead or enjoy artist talks or workshops Mentorship support from noted local photographer, Terry Hastings. Other planned exhibition opportunities may include: Exhibition and reception at the Palm Springs Cultural Center Downtown multimedia projections and installations Construction screening installations Exhibition opportunities with local arts organizations and preservation partners Archival inclusion within regional cultural institutions In addition: One photographer will receive a seasonal “Best of Residency” award and a $500 honorarium Participating hotels will receive a selected print from the residency Select works may become part of future publications documenting the history of Palm Springs boutique hotels Images may appear in citywide promotional installations and future Artist in Residence campaigns Community and Cultural Partnerships This Artist in Residence Program is being developed in collaboration with organizations across the Palm Springs arts, preservation, and hospitality communities, including: Palm Springs Cultural Center Palm Springs Art Museum Architecture and Design Center Palm Springs Public Arts Commission Palm Springs Life Desert Art Center The Hastings Gallery Additional partnerships and sponsors will be announced as the residency launch approaches.

name: Quilt National '27
award_info: Selected award-winning quilts will be acquired by the International Quilt Museum in Lincoln, Nebraska, and added to its Quilt National Collection. The Purchase Award payment serves as both the award honorarium and the purchase price for the quilt. By accepting a Purchase Award, the artist agrees to transfer ownership of the quilt to the International Quilt Museum. Purchase Awards will be announced during opening weekend. Award-winning quilts will remain in the Quilt National exhibition tour for approximately two years before entering the International Quilt Museum’s permanent Quilt National Collection, where they will be preserved for exhibition, study, and research. Best of Show sponsored by MODA, $7,500; Most Innovative Use of Material sponsored by the James Family, $5,000 ; Award of Excellence sponsored by Aurifil, $5,000 ; Emerging Artist sponsored by the Pumphrey Family, $2,500
